design_philosophy.core_loop: Run, jump, and kick through side-scrolling stages while avoiding instant-death hazards (bottomless pits, spikes) and collecting time bonuses to beat the clock
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platformer execution under time pressure; navigating hazard-heavy level design with simplistic combat mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Speedy Gonzales from the cartoon, moving quickly through Mexican-themed levels and defeating iconic Looney Tunes enemies like Sylvester and Robocat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily derivative of Sonic the Hedgehog's momentum-based design philosophy, but executed poorly with unresponsive controls described as 'controlling a large walrus'; emphasizes time management and item collection over skill-based platforming

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses a life upon touching bottomless pits, spikes, or getting locked in cages; abundant time bonuses mitigate difficulty
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Time bonuses provide cushion for casual play; level design difficulty is fixed with no difficulty selection mentioned

progression.upgrade_system: Items can be picked up and held for usage at later points in the game
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based progression (referenced as 'Stage 6-1'); levels unlock sequentially
replayability_hooks: Time bonus collection encourages speedrun playstyle

world_building_techniques: Licensed character presence (Speedy Gonzales, Sylvester, Robocat)

danger_signals: Cage entrapment mechanic causing life loss
environmental_storytelling: Time pressure mechanic reinforcing 'speedy' character concept
